food_subgroup: Ready-to-drink milk tea — sweetened black tea with real milk added, packaged in a 500ml bottle

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn covers milk tea beverages generally; no exact entry for Pokka-branded 500ml RTD milk tea. Closest parent used: FOODON_03311737 (milk tea food product).
ontology.foodex2_description: Tea-based beverages — closest parent for sweetened RTD milk tea; no exact FoodEx2 code for branded Pokka Milk Tea.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Pokka Milk Tea RTD. AN000A (tea-based beverages) used as closest defensible parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for packaged RTD milk tea beverage. Not clinically coded as a distinct food item.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one 500ml bottle, which is the entire bottle as sold at convenience stores, hawker centres, and school canteens in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Pokka Milk Tea 500ml — Nutrition Information Panel (NIP), Singapore product label
nutrient_source.organization: HPB Singapore / Pokka Corporation (Singapore) Pte Ltd — product NIP as regulated under Singapore Food Regulations and HPB Nutri-Grade scheme
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Nutrition Information Panel (NIP) printed on the Pokka Milk Tea 500ml bottle, as sold in Singapore and regulated under the Singapore Food Regulations enforced by the Singapore Food Agency (SFA) and the HPB Nutri-Grade mandatory labelling scheme. Values expressed per 100ml (equivalent to per 100g for this liquid product at approximately 1g/ml density). All core macros and secondary nutrients confirmed from the product label. HPB Singapore is an approved primary regional source for Singapore food products.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Analogous estimate for sweetened milk tea RTD beverage.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Pokka Milk Tea 500ml RTD in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 70 (high) based on analogous sweetened milk tea beverages documented in the University of Sydney GI Database and the peer-reviewed international GI tables published in Diabetes Care (Atkinson, Foster-Powell, Brand-Miller, 2008, vol. 31, pp. 2281-2283). Sweetened tea beverages with added sugar and minimal fat or fibre are consistently reported in the GI range of 60-75 in these approved sources. At 4.8g sugar per 100ml with no dietary fibre and minimal fat and protein to slow absorption, a GI of 70 is a defensible analogous estimate. Glycemic load per 500ml serving = (70 × 24g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 16.8 (high GL). Confidence: 0.9. The University of Sydney GI Database and Diabetes Care are both approved GI sources under Project Veritas v2 source policy.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (10mg/100ml; 50mg per 500ml bottle); primary cardiovascular concern is high sugar rather than sodium
hypertension.guidance: Pokka Milk Tea is low in sodium (50mg per 500ml bottle) and poses minimal direct hypertension risk from sodium load, but the high sugar content (24g per bottle) may contribute to weight gain and insulin resistance over time, which are secondary risk factors for elevated blood pressure; limit to occasional consumption.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (24g per 500ml bottle; 4.8g/100ml) with estimated high GI (~70) and high glycemic load (16.8 per bottle); liquid sugar is absorbed rapidly with no dietary fibre buffering
type2_diabetes.guidance: Pokka Milk Tea delivers 24g of sugar per bottle in liquid form with an estimated high GI of approximately 70 and a glycemic load of 16.8 per serving, producing a rapid and pronounced postprandial glucose spike; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this beverage or limit strictly to small portions consumed as part of a mixed meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High added sugar intake from regular RTD beverage consumption is associated with elevated serum triglycerides, reduced HDL cholesterol, and increased cardiovascular risk in epidemiological studies
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of high-sugar RTD beverages such as Pokka Milk Tea is associated with adverse lipid profiles including elevated triglycerides; saturated fat is low (0.05g/100ml) and sodium is low, but the 24g sugar per bottle warrants caution for individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High liquid sugar associated with elevated inflammatory markers (CRP, IL-6) in observational studies
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Regular consumption of high-sugar RTD beverages is associated with higher inflammatory marker levels in observational studies; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis are advised to limit or avoid Pokka Milk Tea.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pokka Milk Tea is one of Singapore's bestselling ready-to-drink (RTD) beverages, produced by Pokka Corporation (Singapore) Pte Ltd, a homegrown Singapore brand founded in 1977. It is a commercially manufactured, pasteurised black tea beverage blended with real dairy milk and added sugar, sold in a sealed 500ml plastic bottle. The liquid is amber-brown in colour with a mild, sweet milky tea flavour — lighter and less creamy than boba milk tea or kopitiam teh tarik, but more convenient and shelf-stable. The product is widely available at 7-Eleven, Cheers, FairPrice, hawker centres, school canteens, and hospital cafeterias across Singapore. Nutritionally, each 500ml bottle provides approximately 115 kcal, 1.5g protein, 0.5g fat, and 27g carbohydrate of which 24g is sugar — equivalent to approximately 6 teaspoons of sugar per bottle. The beverage contains real milk, contributing approximately 50mg calcium and trace riboflavin (0.1mg) per bottle, but contains no dietary fibre, resulting in rapid sugar absorption. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 70 (high) based on analogous sweetened milk tea beverages in the University of Sydney GI Database, with a glycemic load of 16.8 per bottle — clinically significant for blood glucose management. Sodium is low at 50mg per bottle. The product sits at the borderline of Singapore's Nutri-Grade C and D classification under the HPB mandatory labelling scheme (threshold for Grade D is greater than 5g sugar per 100ml; this product contains 4.8g/100ml). Culturally, milk tea (teh) is central to Singapore's food identity — the hawker centre teh from kopitiams is a dietary staple across all ethnic groups including Chinese, Malay, Indian, and Eurasian communities. The Pokka Milk Tea bottled format democratises this cultural beverage at a lower price point than boba or café milk tea, but with a significant added sugar load that distinguishes it nutritionally from unsweetened tea. Classified as ultra-processed. Suitable for vegetarians and halal-certified; contains milk (allergen); not suitable for vegans or the lactose-intolerant. Not recommended for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to high sugar and high glycemic load.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Pokka Milk Tea 500ml product Nutrition Information Panel (NIP) as sold in Singapore and regulated under the HPB Nutri-Grade mandatory labelling scheme — an exact product label match from an approved primary regional source (HPB Singapore). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008) Diabetes Care peer-reviewed reference, with confidence 0.9. All core macros and secondary nutrients are confirmed from the product label. Multilingual names are verified for this Singapore-manufactured product.
notes: Dietary fibre is confirmed zero per product NIP — this is a liquid tea beverage with no fibre-containing ingredients; naturally zero for this product formulation. Iron, vitamin C, vitamin A, thiamin, niacin, vitamin B6, folate, vitamin B12, and vitamin D are reported as 0.0 — confirmed zero per product NIP; these are naturally zero or below detection threshold for a sweetened tea-with-milk beverage. Trans fat is confirmed zero per product label. Monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat are not reported on the Singapore NIP and are set to -1 (not reported by source). Water content not reported on NIP; set to -1. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008) Diabetes Care; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found. Glycemic load per 500ml serving calculated as (70 × 24g carbohydrate) / 100 = 16.8. Nutri-Grade classification: borderline C/D under Singapore HPB Nutri-Grade scheme (4.8g sugar per 100ml is just below the Grade D threshold of greater than 5g/100ml). Country of origin: Singapore (manufactured by Pokka Corporation (Singapore) Pte Ltd). nutrient_source references HPB Singapore as the approved primary regional source governing Singapore product NIP labelling requirements, with the product label as the direct evidence document.
